name: xarizmi

metadata.name: xarizmi
metadata.summary: Xarizmi (read Khwarizmi) project is an educational project thatcontains tools for technical analysis in Python.

documentation:
# Xarizmi
Xarizmi (read Khwarizmi) project is an educational project that contains tools for technical analysis in Python.


## Installation
```bash
pip install xarizmi
```

## Example

### Build Candlestick
```python
from xarizmi.candlestick import Candlestick
c = Candlestick(
    **{
        "open": 2,
        "low": 1,
        "high": 4,
        "close": 3,
    }
)
```


### Indicators
### OBV Indicator
```python
from xarizmi.candlestick import CandlestickChart
from xarizmi.ta.obv import OBVIndicator

# assuming btc_usdt_monthly_data is defined (similar to tests/conftest.py)
c = CandlestickChart.model_validate({"candles": btc_usdt_monthly_data})

obv_indicator = OBVIndicator(candlestick_chart=c, volume='amount')
obv_indicator.compute()
print(obv_indicator.indicator_data)
obv_indicator.plot()
```

classes.name: xarizmi.models.actors.Actor
documentation: Actor is base class for actors that are involved in the market.

classes.name: xarizmi.models.actors.ExchangeActor
documentation:
ExchangeActor is an actor that plays the role of Exchange.

Exchanges usually have a lot of money and assets. They get fee
per trader and they have the advantage of knowing the orders of
other actors who use their platform to trade or invest.

They also have a lot of money and they can manipulate market in
good or bad ways.

classes.name: xarizmi.models.actors.TraderActor
documentation:
A person who is a trader in the market.
They buy and sell continuously and they hold securities for a
short period of time.

classes.name: xarizmi.models.actors.InvestorActor
documentation: InvestorActor holds securities for a long time.

methods.name: xarizmi.models.symbol.Symbol.build
documentation:
# Example

```pycon
>>> symbol = Symbol.build(
        base_currency="BTC",
        quote_currency="USD",
        fee_currency="USD",
        exchange="BINANCE",
    )
```

methods.name: xarizmi.models.symbol.Symbol.to_string
documentation:
# Example

```pycon
>>> symbol = Symbol.build(
        base_currency="BTC",
        quote_currency="USD",
        fee_currency="USD",
        exchange="BINANCE",
    )
>>> symbol.to_string()
'BTC-USD'
```

methods.name: xarizmi.models.symbol.Symbol.to_flat_csv
documentation:
# Example

```pycon
>>> symbol = Symbol.build(
        base_currency="BTC",
        quote_currency="USD",
        fee_currency="USD",
        exchange="BINANCE",
    )
>>> symbol.to_string()
'BTC-USD'
```

methods.name: xarizmi.models.symbol.Symbol.to_dict
documentation:
# Example

```pycon
>>> symbol = Symbol.build(
        base_currency="BTC",
        quote_currency="USD",
        fee_currency="USD",
        exchange="BINANCE",
    )
>>> symbol.to_dict()
{
    "base_currency": "BTC",
    "quote_currency": "USD",
    "fee_currency": "USD"
    "exchange": "BINANCE",
}
```

classes.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.range
documentation: Range = H - L

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.intrinsic_range
documentation: IR = R / L

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.body
documentation: B = O - C

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.intrinsic_body
documentation: B / R

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.upper_shadow
documentation: US = H - MAX(C, O)

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.intrinsic_upper_shadow
documentation: US / R

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.lower_shadow
documentation: LS = min(C, O) - L

methods.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.intrinsic_lower_shadow
documentation: LS / R

class_variables.name: xarizmi.candlestick.Candlestick.interval
documentation: interval in seconds

This class contains basic properties of a candlestick.

```
param close

the closing price for the corresponding time period

type close

float

param open

the opening price for the corresponding time period

type open

float

param low

the lowest traded price for the corresponding time period

type low

float

param high

the highest traded price for the corresponding time period

type high

float

param volume

the amount of trading volume (width of the candlestick)

type volume

float

param amount

[amount description?]

type amount

float

param interval_type

the time period for each candlestick

type interval_type

str

param symbol

the type of the trade, e.g., "CAD-USD" or "BTC-USDT"

type symbol

str
```

functions.name: xarizmi.db.alembic.env.run_migrations_offline
documentation:
Run migrations in 'offline' mode.

This configures the context with just a URL
and not an Engine, though an Engine is acceptable
here as well.  By skipping the Engine creation
we don't even need a DBAPI to be available.

Calls to context.execute() here emit the given string to the
script output.
other values from the config, defined by the needs of env.py,
can be acquired:
my_important_option = config.get_main_option("my_important_option")
... etc.

functions.name: xarizmi.db.alembic.env.run_migrations_online
documentation:
Run migrations in 'online' mode.

In this scenario we need to create an Engine
and associate a connection with the context.
